channel.title: Frontion News - Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from trusted sources worldwide

item.title: 🇦🇪🇮🇷⚔️ UAE Air Defences Engage Iranian Missiles and Drones as Oil Zone Hit by Fire
item.description: The UAE said its air defences were engaging missile and drone threats as firefighters battled a blaze at a major oil industry zone following a drone attack that authorities said originated from Iran.

item.title: 🇰🇷🚢💥 South Korean-Operated Vessel Ablaze in Strait of Hormuz After Iranian Attack
item.description: A South Korean-operated ship had an explosion and caught fire in the Strait of Hormuz, in an incident which U.S. President Donald Trump said was caused by an Iranian attack.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳🇮🇷 Bessent Urges China to Step Up Diplomacy on Iran Ahead of Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent urged China to intensify diplomatic efforts to persuade Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz to international shipping, saying Trump and Xi will discuss the subject next week.

item.title: 💰📉🇪🇺 Dollar Rises, European Stocks Fall After Reports US Warship Hit by Missiles
item.description: The dollar strengthened while European shares and bond prices fell after Iran Fars News reported two missiles hit a U.S. warship attempting to pass through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚔️🚢 Iran Navy Says It Prevented US Warships From Entering Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Irans navy said it prevented the entry of enemy warships into the Strait of Hormuz by issuing a swift and decisive warning, according to state TV.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korean ship struck by explosion and fire in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Seoul confirmed that a South Korean vessel was hit by an explosion and fire in the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, as Iran launched attacks on shipping in the strategic waterway. The incident underscores the widening impact of the US-Iran conflict on global commercial traffic.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Türkiye navigates post-European security landscape amid Iran war fallout
item.description: While the EU seeks to frame Türkiye within its security architecture, Ankara has moved to project its own cross-regional security framework. The analysis examines strategic positioning as European security structures face unprecedented strain from the Middle East conflict.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Yerevan summit puts Armenia in global spotlight as European leaders converge
item.description: The Armenian capital receives rare global attention as dozens of world leaders and the NATO chief gather for two European-led summits. The meetings come as regional security architecture faces strain from multiple conflicts.

item.title: 🇪🇺 European leaders grapple with US indifference at Armenia summit
item.description: At the European Political Community summit in Yerevan, Macron called US indifference 'the elephant in the room' while Starmer acknowledged Europe's alliances 'are not where we want them to be'. The summit addressed energy security, Ukraine support, and strategic autonomy from the US.

item.title: 🇦🇹 Austria expels three Russian diplomats over espionage operation
item.description: Austria expelled three Russian diplomats after discovering a 'forest of antennas' on diplomatic buildings used to intercept satellite data. The move follows Austria's shift to take stronger action against Russian espionage since the Ukraine war began.

item.title: 🇲🇱 Mali junta leader appoints himself defense minister after predecessor killed in attacks
item.description: Gen Assimi Goita took over Mali's defense ministry after his predecessor was killed in coordinated attacks by jihadist and separatist forces. The attacks have challenged the military government's authority and led to a partial blockade of Bamako.

item.title: 🇨🇦 Alberta separatists submit 300,000 signatures for independence referendum
item.description: The Stay Free Alberta group delivered over 300,000 signatures to trigger a potential independence referendum in October. A legal challenge by First Nations groups argues the separation would violate their treaty rights, with a court decision expected later this month.

item.title: 🇨🇦 Carney says international order could be 'rebuilt out of Europe'
item.description: Canadian PM Mark Carney told the European Political Community summit that Canada would deepen ties with Europe and that a new international order could emerge from the continent. The summit addressed US-Europe tensions over NATO and the Iran war.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇺🇦 Putin and Zelenskiy declare rival ceasefires for Victory Day
item.description: Putin declared a May 8-9 ceasefire for Russia WWII commemorations, while Zelenskiy proposed an earlier pause starting May 5-6, as both sides position for diplomatic leverage.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇩🇪🚗 Trump tariff threats on European cars return with vengeance at 25%
item.description: The administration plans to hike EU auto tariffs to 25% from 15%, citing the EU failure to ratify trade agreements, adding fresh volatility for German automakers already squeezed by whipsawing policies.

item.title: 🇲🇾🇸🇬 Singapore Power Deal Boosts Sarawak Green Energy Ambitions
item.description: Malaysian state on Borneo aims to become the "battery of Southeast Asia" with Singapore power partnership advancing renewable energy development.

item.title: 🇨🇴 Three Dead in Colombia Monster Truck Show Crash
item.description: A monster truck veered out of control at a show in Colombia, killing three including a 10-year-old girl and injuring 38 spectators.

item.title: 🇬🇾 Guyana President: Energy Investment Insufficient as Iran War Strains Global Supply
item.description: President Irfaan Ali warned that the gap between energy supply and demand is widening as the Iran war enters its third month, driving up oil and gas prices amid supply shortages.

item.title: 🚗 German Carmakers Slump as Trump Hikes EU Car Tariffs to 25%
item.description: President Trump raised US tariffs on imported European cars from 15% to 25%, dealing a fresh blow to German automakers already struggling with Middle East supply disruptions.

item.title: ✈️ Embraer Sees New Middle East Defense Deals After UAE C-390 Order
item.description: Brazil Embraer expects additional defense aircraft deals in the Middle East following a landmark UAE order for C-390 military transport planes, with government-to-government procurements in the pipeline.

item.title: 🇸🇪 Sweden Arrests Chinese Captain of Russia-Linked Oil Tanker
item.description: Swedish authorities arrested the Chinese captain of a seized oil tanker with suspected Russian ties on charges of carrying false documents and violating seaworthiness laws, highlighting continued sanctions enforcement.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia Intensifies Drone Attacks on Ukrainian Port Infrastructure
item.description: Kyiv reports Russia has used over 800 drones against Ukrainian ports in the first four months of 2026—a tenfold increase from the same period last year—escalating attacks on critical export infrastructure.

item.title: ⛽ Russia Hits Five Naftogaz Facilities as Energy Infrastructure Targeted
item.description: Russian attacks struck five facilities of Ukraine's state energy company Naftogaz, damaging equipment and causing fires, the CEO reported, continuing Moscow's campaign against Ukrainian energy assets.

item.title: 🇦🇪 UAE Energy Minister: OPEC Exit Not Directed at Anyone
item.description: Following its departure from OPEC, the UAE energy minister said the country owes it to investment partners to produce without restrictions while cooperating with other crude producers.

item.title: 🚗 US to Raise EU Car Tariffs to 25%
item.description: The US Trade Representative confirmed Washington will proceed with President Trump's plan to increase EU car import tariffs to 25%, dealing a blow to German automakers already under pressure.
